features.name: model-inference
features.description: Core LLM inference engine integrating llama.cpp, GGML backends (CUDA, Metal, Vulkan), KV caching, sampling, and multi-modal support for running models locally.

flows.name: generate-text-completion
flows.description: User sends a prompt and the runner streams back generated text tokens via the LLM inference pipeline.

flows.name: generate-text-embedding
flows.description: User submits text and the runner returns a vector embedding instead of generated tokens.

flows.name: run-multimodal-inference
flows.description: User sends a prompt with image or audio inputs and the runner processes multimodal embeddings alongside text for generation.

flows.name: load-model-for-inference
flows.description: The server loads a GGUF model file and initialises the backend, KV cache, and sampler before accepting inference requests.

flows.name: run-inference-flow
flows.description: Execute model inference with generation and embedding endpoints.

features.name: CLI & Desktop Launcher
features.description: Command-line interface providing interactive REPL, model management commands, configuration, and launcher integrations for third-party AI coding tools.

flows.name: run-interactive-chat
flows.description: User starts an interactive REPL session with a model, issuing prompts and slash commands from the terminal.

flows.name: launch-ai-coding-integration
flows.description: User selects and launches a third-party AI coding tool (Claude, Copilot, Codex, Cline, etc.) integrated with Ollama.

flows.name: start-ollama-server
flows.description: User starts the Ollama background server process, with platform-specific handling for Darwin, Windows, and Linux.

flows.name: invoke-cli-command
flows.description: User runs a top-level CLI command (pull, push, run, list, etc.) via the Cobra command tree.

flows.name: launch-desktop-app
flows.description: User opens the native desktop application, which initialises the system-tray app and embedded webview on macOS or Windows.

flows.name: run-benchmark
flows.description: User runs a model benchmark from the CLI to measure inference performance.

flows.name: manage-running-models-flow
flows.description: Start, stop, and list currently running model instances.

features.name: MLX Inference Runner
features.description: Apple Silicon MLX-based inference runner and image generation pipeline, providing hardware-accelerated model execution on macOS via the MLX framework.

flows.name: run-text-inference
flows.description: User starts the MLX runner server to serve a language model and receive streaming text completions.

flows.name: run-image-generation
flows.description: User loads an image generation model and submits prompts to produce images via the MLX subprocess server.

features.name: Model Registry
features.description: Server-side model registry handling model storage, blob management, download/upload, scheduling, quantization, authentication, and the REST API surface for all model operations.

flows.name: pull-model
flows.description: User pulls a model from the remote registry to local storage.

flows.name: push-model
flows.description: User pushes a locally created model up to the remote registry.

flows.name: create-model
flows.description: User creates a new model from a Modelfile, optionally uploading blobs.

flows.name: delete-model
flows.description: User deletes a locally stored model by name.

flows.name: list-local-models
flows.description: User lists all locally available models and their metadata.

flows.name: show-model-details
flows.description: User retrieves detailed information about a specific model including its configuration and template.

flows.name: copy-model
flows.description: User copies an existing local model under a new name.

flows.name: manage-models-flow
flows.description: CRUD operations for model lifecycle: create, list, show, delete, copy, pull, and push.

flows.name: manage-blobs-flow
flows.description: Blob storage operations for model artifacts: create and head checks.

flows.name: model-metadata-flow
flows.description: Retrieve upstream model information and registry metadata.

features.name: Agentic Tool Use
features.description: Agentic tool-use capabilities including web search, browser crawling, and web fetch that models can invoke during inference.

flows.name: execute-tool-via-anthropic
flows.description: User sends a messages request to the Anthropic-compatible API with tool definitions, triggering agentic tool selection and execution.

flows.name: execute-tool-via-openai
flows.description: User sends a chat completion request to the OpenAI-compatible API with tool calls, triggering tool dispatch and response formatting.

flows.name: parse-model-tool-calls
flows.description: Streaming model output is parsed in real time to detect and extract structured tool-call payloads from the token stream.

flows.name: run-agentic-chat
flows.description: User invokes the agent CLI to conduct a multi-turn chat session with tool use and approval gating.

flows.name: register-and-invoke-tool
flows.description: Application registers named tools (web search, browser, fetch) into the registry and invokes them by name during an agentic loop.

flows.name: web-tools-flow
flows.description: Experimental web search, fetch, and browser integration for agentic tool use.

features.name: ui
features.description: Web-based chat UI for interacting with local Ollama models, including the chat view, settings panel, and routing.

flows.name: send-chat-message
flows.description: User selects a model, composes a message with optional file attachments, and sends it to receive a streamed AI response.

flows.name: browse-and-switch-chats
flows.description: User opens the sidebar to view past conversations and navigate between them.

flows.name: configure-settings
flows.description: User opens the settings page to adjust application preferences such as model defaults and behaviour options.

flows.name: launch-home-view
flows.description: User opens the app and is redirected to either the new-chat view or the launch-commands view based on their last home view setting.

flows.name: upload-file-to-chat
flows.description: User drags, drops, or pastes an image or file into the chat input to attach it to their message.

flows.name: chat-via-ui
flows.description: User opens a chat session in the web UI identified by a chat ID to interact with the inference backend.

flows.name: configure-settings-flow
flows.description: User opens Settings in the UI; preferences such as default model and cloud connectivity are read and saved via the settings store.

flows.name: chat-interaction-flow
flows.description: Core chat messaging and inference execution with model capability discovery.

flows.name: chat-persistence-flow
flows.description: Chat history deletion and management.

flows.name: cloud-integration-flow
flows.description: Cloud service connectivity, status, and configuration management.

flows.name: ui-shell-flow
flows.description: Web UI entry point and desktop launcher URL scheme handling.

flows.name: system-status-flow
flows.description: Server health, process status, and inference compute resource monitoring.

flows.name: discover-gpu-devices
flows.description: System discovers and caches available GPU devices along with their VRAM and library paths for scheduling inference.

flows.name: discover-system-cpu-memory
flows.description: System collects CPU details and memory availability (including cgroup constraints) to determine thread count and free memory for CPU inference.

features.name: Model Conversion
features.description: Model conversion pipeline that transforms weights from HuggingFace and other formats into GGUF, including tokenizer conversion and template mapping.

flows.name: convert-model-weights-to-ggml
flows.description: User provides a directory of safetensors or PyTorch model files which are parsed, architecture-detected, and written out as a GGML-compatible format.

flows.name: parse-and-embed-tokenizer
flows.description: User's model directory is scanned for tokenizer.json or SentencePiece vocabulary files, which are parsed and embedded into the converted model artifact.

flows.name: resolve-chat-template
flows.description: User supplies a template name or string and the system resolves it to a built-in chat prompt template for the converted model.

flows.name: create-quantized-model
flows.description: User creates and quantizes a model by converting weights to a target dtype for MLX inference.

features.name: auth
features.description: Authentication and account access — sign-in, sign-up, password recovery, email verification, session management.

flows.name: authenticate-cloud-account
flows.description: User sees the login prompt and signs in to their cloud account when the app detects they are not authenticated.

flows.name: authenticate-user
flows.description: User signs in or out of the Ollama registry to enable push/pull of private models.

flows.name: manage-auth-flow
flows.description: Sign-in and sign-out operations for CLI user sessions.

flows.name: session-management-flow
flows.description: User authentication state: sign-out and current user identity retrieval.
